seoTitle: How to Run an Accounts Receivable Report in QBO
metaDescription: Learn how to generate an Accounts Receivable report in QuickBooks Online to see exactly what customers owe you and stay on top of cash flow.
intro: Knowing what customers still owe you is essential for managing cash flow, and QuickBooks Online's Accounts Receivable report makes that visible in a few clicks. This guide covers how to run and filter the report to get a clear picture of outstanding balances.

steps.heading: Open the Reports menu
steps.body: Go to Reports and search for the Accounts Receivable report, sometimes listed as A/R Aging.

steps.heading: Choose a summary or detail view
steps.body: Select whether you want a summary of totals owed or a detailed breakdown by invoice and customer.

steps.heading: Apply filters
steps.body: Filter by customer, date, or aging period so the report focuses on the balances most relevant to you.

steps.heading: Review and act on the report
steps.body: Use the report to identify overdue balances and follow up with customers as needed.

faq.question: What's the difference between A/R summary and detail reports?
faq.answer: The summary shows total balances by customer, while the detail report breaks down each individual open invoice.

faq.question: Can I see how overdue an invoice is?
faq.answer: Yes, the A/R aging report groups outstanding invoices into time buckets like current, 30 days, 60 days, and beyond.

faq.question: Can I export the Accounts Receivable report?
faq.answer: Yes, you can export the report to Excel or PDF directly from the report screen.

faq.question: How often should I check this report?
faq.answer: Many businesses review it weekly or monthly to stay ahead of overdue balances and manage cash flow.
